titleStmt.title: Letter from Administrative Area Officer, Royal Hospital Kilmainham, to the Chief Crown Solicitor, 10 March 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: A colonel

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from the Area Administrative Superintendent of the Royal Hospital Dublin to the Chief Crown Solicitor of Ireland, Malchy Kelly (1850-1916). The letter concerns the injury of Patrick Sheehan (1879-?), a cork labourer who dislocated his ankle while carrying sacks while working. The letter states that the accident happened during Sheehan's ordinary course of duties.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/3785

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Ref. No. 92/
Subject. Injury to Patrick Sheehan A.A.O., Ireland
Royal Hospital,
Dublin. 10th. March, 1916. The Chief Crown Solicitor,
Dublin. Sir, In reply to yours of 8.3.16, with
reference to Patrick Sheehan. This man’s
accident occurred in the ordinary course of his
duties. He was employed by the Forage Dept.
for casual labour, and in the ordinary way would
have to handle hay, straw, or oats, as required.
The bags he was carrying were oat bags. Yours faithfully, GM Colonel,
ADMINISTRATIVE AREA OFFICER
G.B./ Solicitor General
In conjunction with your remit
18.3.16
M. Kelly
11.3.16 The permit is not free from
doubt: but I think the
war depot is liable to pay
compensation
EOF
12.3.16 Administration Area Office
Please see Solicitor General's opinion above
M - Kelly
13.3.16
